question: [File: 'src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: High Incorrect loss-factor accounting causing unfair debt reduction, credit destruction, bad debt misallocation, or position desynchronization'] [Function: safeTransferFrom / repay + lastLossFactor desync] Can an unprivileged borrower repay() with a fee-on-transfer loan token to inflate withdrawable and totalUnits, then a liquidation updates lossFactor using the inflated totalUnits (producing a smaller increment), and when a lender calls updatePosition(), their credit is slashed by the under-computed lossFactor delta, leaving them with more credit than they should have after the true bad debt, so they can withdraw more tokens than the protocol can cover, draining funds from other lenders? Preconditions: fee-on-transfer loan token; lender has credit; bad debt event. Call sequence: (1) repay() inflates totalUnits; (2) liquidate() under-updates lossFactor; (3) lender updatePosition() applies under-slash; (4) lender withdraw(over-credited amount) drains other lenders. Invariant tested: bad_debt_reduces_lender_credit_exactly_once_and_proportionally. Scoped impact: unfair debt reduction; other lenders' credit destruction. Proof idea: fuzz inflation and bad debt; assert sum of all lender credits after slash == totalUnits.

[File: 'src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: High Incorrect loss-factor accounting causing unfair debt reduction, credit destruction, bad debt misallocation, or position desynchronization'] [Function: safeTransferFrom / repay + continuousFeeCredit over-scaling] Can an unprivileged borrower repay() with a fee-on-transfer loan token to inflate withdrawable, then a liquidation with badDebt > 0 scales continuousFeeCredit down using the formula continuousFeeCredit.mulDivDown(type(uint128).max - newLossFactor, type(uint128).max - oldLossFactor), where newLossFactor is under-computed due to inflated totalUnits, resulting in continuousFeeCredit being scaled down less than it should be, so feeClaimer can claim more continuousFeeCredit than the true backing, draining lender withdrawable? Preconditions: fee-on-transfer loan token; continuousFeeCredit > 0; bad debt event. Call sequence: (1) repay() inflates totalUnits; (2) liquidate() under-updates lossFactor; (3) continuousFeeCredit scaled down less; (4) claimContinuousFee(excess) drains withdrawable. Invariant tested: continuousFeeCredit must not exceed actual backing. Scoped impact: insolvency; lender credit destruction. Proof idea: fuzz continuousFeeCredit and fee rate; assert claimContinuousFee does not reduce withdrawable below lender credit sum.

[File: 'src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: High Incorrect loss-factor accounting causing unfair debt reduction, credit destruction, bad debt misallocation, or position desynchronization'] [Function: safeTransferFrom / take + fee-on-transfer + offer consumption] Can an unprivileged taker use a fee-on-transfer loan token in take() where offer.maxAssets > 0, so that consumed[maker][group] += buyerAssets (full amount) but the actual tokens transferred are buyerAssets - fee, and on a subsequent take() the consumption check newConsumed <= offer.maxAssets passes because consumed tracks the full buyerAssets, but the actual backing for the credit created is less, causing the total credit issued to exceed the actual token balance? Preconditions: fee-on-transfer loan token; offer.maxAssets > 0; multiple partial fills. Call sequence: (1) take1() consumed += buyerAssets1; (2) take2() consumed += buyerAssets2; (3) total credit > actual balance. Invariant tested: offer fill amount cannot exceed remaining offer capacity; tokenBalanceCorrect. Scoped impact: credit over-issuance; insolvency. Proof idea: fuzz N partial fills with fee token; assert sum(credit) <= balance.

[File: 'src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: High Incorrect loss-factor accounting causing unfair debt reduction, credit destruction, bad debt misallocation, or position desynchronization'] [Function: safeTransferFrom / repay + isHealthy check bypass] Can an unprivileged borrower repay() with a fee-on-transfer loan token to reduce their recorded debt (position[id][onBehalf].debt -= units) while only delivering units - fee to the contract, then call withdrawCollateral() which calls isHealthy() using the reduced debt and full recorded collateral, allowing the borrower to withdraw more collateral than they should be able to, leaving the position undercollateralized and creating bad debt that inflates lossFactor incorrectly? Preconditions: fee-on-transfer loan token; borrower near health threshold; repay reduces debt. Call sequence: (1) repay(units) reduces debt by units; (2) isHealthy passes with reduced debt; (3) withdrawCollateral(excess) succeeds; (4) position becomes undercollateralized; (5) liquidate() realizes bad debt. Invariant tested: collateral cannot be withdrawn if it would make debt unhealthy. Scoped impact: bad debt creation; lossFactor corruption. Proof idea: fuzz repay amount and collateral withdrawal; assert isHealthy after withdrawCollateral.

[File: 'src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: High Incorrect loss-factor accounting causing unfair debt reduction, credit destruction, bad debt misallocation, or position desynchronization'] [Function: safeTransferFrom / liquidate + fee-on-transfer + RCF check] Can an unprivileged liquidator call liquidate() in normal mode where the RCF check uses _position.debt (already reduced by badDebt) and maxDebt computed from recorded collateral (inflated by fee-on-transfer), so maxRepaid = (_position.debt - maxDebt).mulDivUp(WAD * WAD, WAD * WAD - lif * lltv) is computed with an inflated maxDebt, allowing the liquidator to repay more than the true RCF allows, over-liquidating the position and leaving it with less debt than the true health threshold, causing position desynchronization? Preconditions: fee-on-transfer collateral token; borrower near health threshold; normal mode liquidation. Call sequence: (1) supplyCollateral(assets) inflates recorded collateral; (2) price drops; (3) liquidate() computes maxDebt with inflated collateral; (4) maxRepaid inflated; (5) over-liquidation. Invariant tested: repay_amount_capped_by_recovery_close_factor. Scoped impact: position desynchronization; over-liquidation. Proof idea: fuzz fee rate and collateral; assert post-liquidation health.

[File: 'src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: High Incorrect loss-factor accounting causing unfair debt reduction, credit destruction, bad debt misallocation, or position desynchronization'] [Function: safeTransferFrom / repay + totalUnits invariant break] Can an unprivileged borrower call repay() with a fee-on-transfer loan token N times to inflate withdrawable by N * fee, causing totalUnits = sumDebt + withdrawable to exceed the actual loan token balance by N * fee, and then when all borrowers repay their debt and all lenders withdraw their credit, the last lender's withdraw() reverts because the contract's balance is insufficient, effectively destroying the last lender's credit without any lossFactor update? Preconditions: fee-on-transfer loan token; N borrowers each repay once; all lenders try to withdraw. Call sequence: (1) N repay() calls inflate withdrawable; (2) all borrowers' debt = 0; (3) lenders withdraw in order; (4) last lender's withdraw reverts. Invariant tested: every credit unit must be redeemable; totalUnitsEqualsSumNegativeDebtPlusWithdrawable. Scoped impact: credit destruction; fund freeze. Proof idea: fuzz N and fee rate; assert all lenders can withdraw.

[File: 'src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: High Incorrect loss-factor accounting causing unfair debt reduction, credit destruction, bad debt misallocation, or position desynchronization'] [Function: safeTransferFrom / take + ERC777 hook reentrancy] Can an unprivileged taker use an ERC777-like loan token that calls a hook on safeTransferFrom, where the hook reenters take() or liquidate() before the first take() completes its state updates (specifically before the seller's health check at line 476), causing the inner call to observe a partially updated state where sellerPos.debt is already incremented but the health check has not yet run, allowing the inner call to create additional debt or trigger a premature liquidation that corrupts lossFactor? Preconditions: ERC777-like loan token with transfer hook; hook reenters take() or liquidate(); seller has debt near health threshold. Call sequence: (1) outer take() updates positions; (2) safeTransferFrom triggers hook; (3) hook reenters take() or liquidate(); (4) inner call observes partial state; (5) lossFactor corrupted. Invariant tested: callbacks must not corrupt partial state through reentrancy. Scoped impact: position desynchronization; lossFactor corruption. Proof idea: implement ERC777 hook that reenters; assert state consistency after sequence.

[File: 'src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: High Incorrect loss-factor accounting causing unfair debt reduction, credit destruction, bad debt misallocation, or position desynchronization'] [Function: safeTransferFrom / repay + fee-on-transfer + gate bypass] Can an unprivileged borrower in a gated market call repay() with a fee-on-transfer loan token to reduce their debt (bypassing the enterGate since repay does not check enterGate), while the fee-on-transfer means the contract receives less than repaid, and then the inflated withdrawable allows a lender to withdraw more than the true repaid amount, effectively bypassing the gate's intent to restrict credit redemption? Preconditions: gated market; fee-on-transfer loan token; borrower has debt; lender has credit. Call sequence: (1) repay() reduces debt, inflates withdrawable; (2) lender withdraw() succeeds with inflated withdrawable; (3) gate not checked for withdrawal. Invariant tested: exit paths must remain available even when enter gate reverts; but withdrawal must not exceed true repaid amount. Scoped impact: insolvency; gate bypass via inflated withdrawable. Proof idea: deploy gated market with fee-on-transfer token; assert withdrawable == actual balance after repay.

[File: 'src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: High Incorrect loss-factor accounting causing unfair debt reduction, credit destruction, bad debt misallocation, or position desynchronization'] [Function: safeTransferFrom / repay + fee-on-transfer + maturity boundary] Can an unprivileged borrower call repay() with a fee-on-transfer loan token at exactly block.timestamp == market.maturity, inflating withdrawable, and then immediately after maturity a liquidation in postMaturityMode uses the inflated totalUnits to compute lossFactor, causing the post-maturity lossFactor ramp (lif = WAD + (_maxLif - WAD) * (block.timestamp - market.maturity) / TIME_TO_MAX_LIF) to be applied to an under-computed lossFactor, resulting in lenders being under-slashed for post-maturity bad debt? Preconditions: fee-on-transfer loan token; repay at maturity boundary; post-maturity liquidation. Call sequence: (1) warp to maturity; (2) repay() inflates withdrawable; (3) warp to maturity + 1; (4) liquidate(postMaturityMode=true) uses inflated totalUnits. Invariant tested: maturity boundary must not allow incorrect settlement. Scoped impact: bad debt misallocation; lender under-slashing. Proof idea: fuzz timestamp at maturity boundary; assert lossFactor correctness.

[File: 'src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: High Incorrect loss-factor accounting causing unfair debt reduction, credit destruction, bad debt misallocation, or position desynchronization'] [Function: safeTransferFrom / repay + fee-on-transfer + dust threshold] Can an unprivileged borrower repay() with a fee-on-transfer loan token where units == 1 wei and the fee makes the actual received amount 0, but position[id][onBehalf].debt -= 1 and marketState[id].withdrawable += 1 are both applied, creating a 1-unit inflation of withdrawable per call, and over many such dust repay() calls the cumulative inflation causes totalUnits to significantly exceed the actual balance, corrupting all future lossFactor calculations? Preconditions: fee-on-transfer loan token with fee >= 1 wei on 1 wei transfer; borrower has debt >= N. Call sequence: (1) N repay(1 wei) calls; (2) withdrawable += N but balance unchanged; (3) liquidate() uses inflated totalUnits. Invariant tested: totalUnitsEqualsSumNegativeDebtPlusWithdrawable with actual balance. Scoped impact: lossFactor corruption via dust inflation. Proof idea: fuzz N dust repays; assert balance == withdrawable + collateralSum.

[File: 'src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: High Incorrect loss-factor accounting causing unfair debt reduction, credit destruction, bad debt misallocation, or position desynchronization'] [Function: safeTransferFrom / liquidate + fee-on-transfer + seizedAssets path] Can an unprivileged liquidator call liquidate() with seizedAssets > 0 and repaidUnits == 0, where repaidUnits is computed as seizedAssets.mulDivUp(liquidatedCollatPrice, ORACLE_PRICE_SCALE).mulDivUp(WAD, lif), and then safeTransferFrom(loanToken, payer, address(this), repaidUnits) with a fee-on-transfer loan token delivers repaidUnits - fee, while _marketState.withdrawable += repaidUnits and _position.debt -= repaidUnits are applied in full, causing withdrawable to be inflated by fee and the debt reduction to exceed the actual tokens received? Preconditions: fee-on-transfer loan token; liquidator uses seizedAssets input path; repaidUnits computed from seizedAssets. Call sequence: (1) liquidate(seizedAssets, 0); (2) repaidUnits computed; (3) safeTransferFrom delivers repaidUnits - fee; (4) withdrawable inflated; (5) debt reduced by full repaidUnits. Invariant tested: debt reduction must correspond to actual token receipt. Scoped impact: position desynchronization; withdrawable inflation. Proof idea: fuzz seizedAssets and fee rate; assert balance == withdrawable after liquidation.

[File: 'src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: High Incorrect loss-factor accounting causing unfair debt reduction, credit destruction, bad debt misallocation, or position desynchronization'] [Function: safeTransferFrom / repay + fee-on-transfer + authorized operator] Can an unprivileged authorized operator call repay(market, units, onBehalf, address(0), '') on behalf of a borrower using a fee-on-transfer loan token, where the operator is the payer (msg.sender), so the operator's balance decreases by units but the contract receives units - fee, while the borrower's debt decreases by units and withdrawable increases by units, effectively allowing the operator to reduce the borrower's debt by more than the actual tokens delivered, creating a withdrawable surplus that corrupts lossFactor? Preconditions: fee-on-transfer loan token; operator authorized by borrower; operator has sufficient balance. Call sequence: (1) borrower authorizes operator; (2) operator calls repay(units, borrower); (3) operator pays units, contract receives units - fee; (4) borrower debt -= units; (5) withdrawable inflated. Invariant tested: every debt reduction must correspond to actual token receipt. Scoped impact: position desynchronization; lossFactor corruption. Proof idea: fuzz units and fee rate with authorized operator; assert balance == withdrawable.

[File: 'src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: High Incorrect loss-factor accounting causing unfair debt reduction, credit destruction, bad debt misallocation, or position desynchronization'] [Function: safeTransferFrom / repay + fee-on-transfer + MidnightBundles] Can an unprivileged borrower use MidnightBundles.repayAndWithdrawCollateral() with a fee-on-transfer loan token, where the bundler calls SafeTransferLib.safeTransferFrom(token, from, address(this), amount) to pull tokens and then calls IMidnight(MIDNIGHT).repay() which calls safeTransferFrom again, causing a double-fee deduction while the accounting only records one repay, or alternatively the bundler's internal balance tracking diverges from Midnight's withdrawable, causing position desynchronization? Preconditions: fee-on-transfer loan token; borrower uses MidnightBundles; referralFeePct > 0. Call sequence: (1) bundler pulls assets via safeTransferFrom (fee deducted); (2) bundler calls repay(units) on Midnight (fee deducted again); (3) Midnight withdrawable += units but received units - fee; (4) bundler balance desync. Invariant tested: ERC20 transfer deltas must match accounting deltas. Scoped impact: position desynchronization; insolvency. Proof idea: fuzz referralFeePct and fee rate; assert Midnight balance == withdrawable after bundler call.

[File: 'src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: High Incorrect loss-factor accounting causing unfair debt reduction, credit destruction, bad debt misallocation, or position desynchronization'] [Function: safeTransferFrom / take + fee-on-transfer + sellerCreditDecrease path] Can an unprivileged taker use a fee-on-transfer loan token in take() where the seller has existing credit (sellerCreditDecrease > 0), so sellerPos.credit -= sellerCreditDecrease and safeTransferFrom(loanToken, payer, receiver, sellerAssets) delivers sellerAssets - fee to receiver, while the seller's credit is reduced by the full sellerCreditDecrease, causing the seller to lose credit without receiving the full corresponding token value, and the fee amount is effectively stolen from the seller? Preconditions: fee-on-transfer loan token; seller has credit; offer.buy == false; sellerCreditDecrease > 0. Call sequence: (1) take() reduces seller credit by sellerCreditDecrease; (2) safeTransferFrom delivers sellerAssets - fee to receiver; (3) seller net loss = fee. Invariant tested: every credit decrease must correspond to correct asset transfer to receiver. Scoped impact: unfair credit destruction; seller loses fee amount. Proof idea: assert receiver balance delta == sellerAssets for normal token; assert delta == sellerAssets - fee for fee token.

[File: 'src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: High Incorrect loss-factor accounting causing unfair debt reduction, credit destruction, bad debt misallocation, or position desynchronization'] [Function: safeTransferFrom / repay + fee-on-transfer + multiple markets same loan token] Can an unprivileged borrower repay() with a fee-on-transfer loan token in market A to inflate market A's withdrawable, and since claimableSettlementFee[loanToken] is shared across all markets using the same loan token, the inflated balance in market A allows claimSettlementFee() to drain tokens that should back market B's withdrawable, causing market B's lenders to be unable to withdraw their credit without any lossFactor update in market B? Preconditions: fee-on-transfer loan token shared across two markets; repay in market A inflates withdrawable; claimSettlementFee drains shared balance. Call sequence: (1) repay(market_A, units) inflates market_A.withdrawable; (2) claimSettlementFee(loanToken, amount) drains shared balance; (3) market_B lender withdraw() fails. Invariant tested: tokenBalanceCorrect: balance >= sum over all markets of withdrawable + collateralSum + claimableSettlementFee. Scoped impact: cross-market insolvency; credit destruction without lossFactor update. Proof idea: two markets same loan token; fuzz repay and claim amounts; assert both markets' lenders can withdraw.
